source.title: Dissociation of reward anticipation and outcome with event-related fMRI.

confidence_rationale: Direct anatomical dissociation of anticipation (ventral striatum / NAcc) vs outcome (vmPFC) circuits within the same subjects, same task. This separates excitement-anticipation from liking-receipt at the brain level; smaller dataset (n=9) but the pattern is robust and replicated by Knutson 2003. Mapping to REE's wanting/liking architecture is direct: NAcc-anticipation = candidate VALENCE_EXCITEMENT; vmPFC-receipt = VALENCE_LIKING.
failure_signatures: Both anticipation and outcome activated dopaminergic projection regions, but distinct foci (ventral striatum vs vmPFC). A single 'positive affect' channel could not fit both signatures.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Reward anticipation activates ventral striatum / NAcc; reward outcome activates ventromedial frontal cortex. The two phases of reward processing recruit anatomically distinct regions along the dopaminergic projection trajectory.
mapping.ree_translation: REE's existing VALENCE_LIKING channel maps to the vmPFC-outcome signature (consummatory hedonic at receipt). The NAcc-anticipation signature is unmapped in current SD-014 and is the candidate VALENCE_EXCITEMENT. Adding the 5th channel would complete the wanting/liking/excitement triad that the human neuroimaging literature already separates.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Same caveats as Knutson 2001a; the anticipation/outcome distinction is well-replicated but the REE residue-field translation requires interpretive work. n=9 in original paper.
mapping.source_context: Healthy adult humans, fMRI during MID task, anticipation epoch (cue) vs outcome epoch (reward delivery) contrasted.

confidence_components.notes: Smaller n than the J Neurosci foundational, but the dissociation pattern is core to the REE architectural question. Mapping fidelity is high because the wanting/liking distinction is already in REE.
